# Page 3^page=3
3 European foreword This document (prEN 18222:2025) has been prepared by Technical Committee CEN/CLC/JTC 24 "Digital Product Passport – Framework and System", the secretariat of which is held by DIN. This document is currently submitted to the CEN Enquiry. This document has been prepared under a standardization request addressed to CEN by the European Commission. The Standing Committee of the EFTA States subsequently approves these requests for its Member States. For the relationship with EU Legislation, see informative Annex ZA, which is an integral part of this document.

# Page 4^page=4
prEN 18222:2025(en) 4 Introduction The Ecodesign Sustainability Product Regulation (ESPR) is an initiative by the European Commission aimed at promoting sustainable products by setting comprehensive requirements for product design, production, and lifecycle management. Central to this initiative is the digital product passport (DPP), which tracks and provides essential information about a product's sustainability attributes. Beside of that also other data can be stored in a DPP depending on future delegated acts and the needs of economic operators. This document introduces the specifications for the API of the DPP, as mandated by the ESPR. The API is designed to enhance the searchability of DPPs and to support interactions throughout the lifecycle of a product's DPP. Furthermore, it also provides an API to register a DPP at the DPP registry. The API methods are presented on a technology-neutral level, detailing the expected inputs and outputs for each method. A detailed technological implementation using a REST-HTTP API is described in the Clause 8, providing guidelines for developers to implement the functionality effectively within their specific environments. The specification document of prEN 18223 “System Interoperability” describes the logical content of the payload that is used by the API. prEN 18216 “Data Exchange Protocols” defines the basic principles of the exchange protocol and the allowed serialization formats of the payload data. Security requirements need to be followed based on the specification document of prEN 18246 .

# Page 5^page=5
5 1 Scope This document aims to standardize the specifications for the API of the digital product passport (DPP) as mandated by the ESPR of the European Commission. The purpose of this API is to facilitate the searchability of DPPs, as well as to provide the necessary means for interactions throughout the lifecycle of a product's DPP. 2 Normative references The following documents are referred to in the text in such a way that some or all of their content constitutes requirements of this document. For dated references, only the edition cited applies. For undated references, the latest edition of the referenced document (including any amendments) applies. prEN 18216, Digital product passport - Data exchange protocols prEN 18221, Digital product passport - data storage, archiving, and data persistence prEN 18223, Digital Product Passport - System interoperability prEN 18223, Digital Product Passport - System interoperability prEN 18223, Digital Product Passport - System interoperability prEN 18246, Digital product passport - Data authentication, reliability and integrity 3 Terms and definitions For the purposes of this document, the following terms and definitions apply. ISO and IEC maintain terminology databases for use in standardization at the following addresses: — ISO Online browsing platform: available at http://www.iso.org/obp — IEC Electropedia: available at http://www.electropedia.org/ 3.1 application programming interface API set of methods provided by an application component for use by other application components 3.1.1 representational state transfer application programming interface REST API service that allows for interaction with resources via a stateless, client-server architecture, typically using standard HTTP calls like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE to perform methods by an application component on these resources 3.2 digital product passport DPP digital record of product characteristics throughout its life cycle

# Page 6^page=6
prEN 18222:2025(en) 6 Note 1 to entry: Example characteristics include environmental sustainability, environmental impact, and recyclability 3.3 method particular way used to perform a specific action on a resource EXAMPLE Read a DPP. 3.4 parameter data provided by a client, that is needed to perform a method EXAMPLE DPP ID for read DPP. 3.5 result return value after the execution of a method EXAMPLE The DPP. 3.6 unique registration identifier identifier returned after uploading the unique identifiers and any other data required to the DPP registry unique identifiers can be unique product identifier, unique facility identifier, unique operator identifier of the economic operator etc. 4 Specification of the Life Cycle API (Main Methods) 4.1 General In this clause only the API methods as can be made available by the custodian of a DPP (either economic operator or service provider) is contained. These methods are specified in abstract manner, the precise implementation is provided in Clause 8, which also contains information how the input and output parameters are provided. The following methods shall be made available by the economic operator or their main digital product passport service provider as well as by back-up digital product passport service provider: — ReadDPPById — ReadDPPByProductId — ReadDPPIdsByProductIds The following methods should be made available by the economic operator or their main digital product passport service provider as well as by back-up digital product passport service provider: — ReadDataElement

# Page 7^page=7
7 The following methods should be made available by the main digital product passport service provider as well as by back-up digital product passport service provider: — CreateDPP — DeleteDPPById — ReadDPPVersionByIdAndDate The following methods shall be made available by the economic operator or their main digital product passport service provider if authorized third parties have the access rights to update parts of the DPP: — UpdateDPPById The following methods should be made available by the economic operator or their main digital product passport service provider if authorized third parties have the access rights to update parts of the DPP: — UpdateDataElement In principle, the DPP API will offer these set of methods, that is requested with some parameters, performing a behaviour (e.g. execution an action), and responding with a result. These expectations are formally described for each method in the following subclauses. Following methods support querying that may be used to create search functionality - ReadDPPByProductId - ReadDPPIdsByProductIds - ReadDPPVersionByProductIdAndDate Content negotiation according Error! Reference source not found. should be used if multiple response types are supported. 4.2 Method ReadDPPById This method returns the DPP through a known DPP identifier. See Table 1. Access rules define the data elements of DPP to be returned in the response. For details see Error! Reference source not found. Table 1 — ReadDPPById Method Name ReadDPPById Explanation Returns the DPP with the specified DPP ID Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter digitalProductPas sportId The DPP’s unique ID yes Identifier 1 Output Parameter

# Page 8^page=8
prEN 18222:2025(en) 8 Method Name ReadDPPById statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 payload Requested DPP yes DigitalProductPas sport 0..1 4.3 Method ReadDPPByProductId This method returns the current active DPP (latest version) through the unique product identifier as defined in Error! Reference source not found.. See Table 2. Access rules define the data elements of DPP to be returned in the response. For details see Error! Reference source not found. Table 2 — ReadDPPByProductId Method Name ReadDPPByProductId Explanation Returns the current active DPP with the specified Product ID Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter productId Product ID Unique product identifier as defined in Error! Reference source not found. yes Identifier 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 payload Requested DPP yes DigitalProductPas sport 0..1 4.4 Method ReadDPPVersionByIdAndDate This method returns a DPP based on its digital product passport identifier and given date. The method is optional for the economic operator. See Table 3. Access rules define the data elements of DPP to be returned in the response. For details see Error! Reference source not found. Table 3 — ReadDPPVersionByIdAndDate Method Name ReadDPPVersionByIdAndDate Explanation Returns a DPP version based on its digitalProductPassportId and given date Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter

# Page 9^page=9
9 Method Name ReadDPPVersionByIdAndDate digitalProductPas sportId DPP ID yes Identifier 1 date Date value for which the DPP is requested yes Timestamp (UTC- based) 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 payload Requested DPP yes DigitalProductPas sport 0..1 Dateformat see Error! Reference source not found. and prEN 18223 4.5 Method ReadDPPIdsByProductIds This method returns a list of DPP identifiers that matches a set of product identifiers. The input parameters "limit" and "cursor" can be set by the client to control pagination. The value of the cursor shall not be empty. See Table 4. Table 4 — ReadDPPIdsByProductIds Method Name ReadDPPIdsByProductIds Explanation Returns a list of DPP identifiers based on discovery information Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter productId Product IDs Unique product identifiers as defined in Error! Reference source not found. yes Identifier[] 1..* Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 payload DPP Identifiers of all DPPs matching the discovery information yes Identifier[] 0..* Identifier[] is a placeholder for an array.

# Page 10^page=10
prEN 18222:2025(en) 10 4.6 Method CreateDPP Method to create a new DPP. Access rules control whether the creation is allowed or not. For details see Error! Reference source not found.. It returns the ID of the DPP. See Table 5. Table 5 — CreateDPP Method Name CreateDPP Explanation Creates DPP Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter dpp DPP yes DigitalProductPas sport 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 digitalProductPas sportId DPP ID yes Identifier 0..1 4.7 Method UpdateDPPById Updates the content of the DPP. The submitted DPP only contains data that needs to be updated or is extended with new data. It can contain a single data element or can be a set of different element information that needs to be updated. See Table 6. Access rules control whether the patch is allowed or not. For details see Error! Reference source not found. If the update of some parts fails the complete update process will fail and there should be no changes adopted in the DPP. All changes to the digital product passport shall be archived in accordance with prEN 18221 . Table 6 — UpdateDPPById Method Name UpdateDPPById Explanation Partial update of a DPP with a specified DPP ID Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter digitalProductPas sportId DPP identifier yes Identifier 1 partialDPP DigitalProductPas sport (only contains parts that needs to be updated) yes DigitalProductPas sport 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code (should yes StatusCode 1

# Page 11^page=11
11 Method Name UpdateDPPById reflect the information when the update fails) payload Updated DPP yes DigitalProductPas sport 0..1 4.8 Method DeleteDPPById Method that removes a DPP of a specific DPP ID. This gets relevant for the end of life of a DPP. Access rules control whether deletion of DPPs is allowed or not (For details see Error! Reference source not found.). See Table 7. Table 7 — DeleteDPPById Method Name DeleteDPPById Explanation Deletes DPP with the specified DPP ID Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter digitalProductPas sportId DPP identifier yes Identifier 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 5 Specification of the DPP Registry API for the Registration Process 5.1 General This clause covers access to external methods of the DPP registry. The DPP registry shall provide the following method: - RegisterProductDPP 5.2 Method RegisterProductDPP This method enables the registration of a new DPP at the DPP registry. This method is served by the DPP registry server. It returns a unique registry identifier at the requesting client. See Table 8. Table 8 — RegisterProductDPP Method Name RegisterProductDPP Explanation Register a new DPP at the registry Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter

# Page 12^page=12
prEN 18222:2025(en) 12 Method Name RegisterProductDPP dppRegistryEntry Contains information relevant for registration yes DppRegistryEntry 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 registrationId unique registration identifier yes String 0..1 6 Specification of the Fine Granular API Methods of the Life Cycle API 6.1 General The operations in this clause may be made available by custodians of DPPs or archiving services. 6.2 Method ReadDataElement This method allows to return a specific data element by its unique identifier path of a specific DPP. See Table 9. Table 9 — ReadDataElement Method Name ReadDataElement Explanation Returns a specific data element by providing the absolute path of the data element Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter digitalProductPas sportId DPP identifier yes Identifier 1 elementIdPath Path to the specific data element. yes String 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 payload Requested data element. yes DataElement 1 6.3 Method UpdateDataElement This method updates a specific data element of a DPP. The method also allows to amend or remove data element information. See Table 10.

# Page 13^page=13
13 Table 10 — UpdateDataElement Method Name UpdateDataElement Explanation Update of a data element within a specific DPP. Name Description Mandatory Type Cardinality Input Parameter digitalProductPas sportId DPP identifier of the DPP where the specific data element needs to be updated yes Identifier 1 elementIdPath Path to the specific data element. yes String 1 payload Content of data that needs to be updated yes DataElement 1 Output Parameter statusCode Response status code yes StatusCode 1 payload Changed data element yes DataElement 1 6.4 According 29.10.2025: REMOVE 6.5 According 29.10.2025: REMOVE 7 Parameter types 7.1 Data types The table below provides information on the types used for life cycle, registration, and fine granular API methods. Table 11 — Data types Type Explanation Identifier Identifier as String as defined in prEN 18223 StatusCode StatusCode based on Table 15 in Clause 7 DppRegistryEntry Digital product passport header as Object including key-value pairs: — uniqueProductIdentifier as String — digitalProductPassportId as String — uniqueEconomicOperatorIdentifier as String

# Page 14^page=14
prEN 18222:2025(en) 14 — uniqueEconomicOperatorIdentifier of the Back-up operator as String — dppApiEndpoint as Uri DigitalProductPassport Digital product passport as Object based on prEN 18223 as well as any DataElement subsets of the product DigitalProductPassport to manage updates. Timestamp dateTime format based on ISO 8601 according prEN18223 DataElement dataElement defined in prEN18223 7.2 Status Code, Error Handling and Result Messages In case of a failed Method execution, a result object should be returned containing more information about the reasons why the Method failed to execute. In case of a failed request, response may only include error object. See Table 12 to Table 15 . Table 12 — General result object Class Name Result Explanation The result object Inherits from  —  semanticId https://jtc24/dpp/API/DataTypes/Result Attribute Explanation Type Cardinality message Additional message containing information for the requester Message 0..* Table 13 defines the structure of the message in the general result object. Table 13 — Message structure Class Name Message Explanation A message containing more information for the requester about a certain happening in the backend Inherits from  —  Attribute Explanation Type Cardinality messageType The message type MessageTypeEnum 1 text The message text as defined in Table 15 String 1

# Page 15^page=15
15 code Technology-dependent status or error code as defined in Table 15 String 0..1 correlationId Identifier to relate several result messages throughout several systems String 0..1 timestamp Timestamp of the message DateTime 0..1 Table 14 defines the value range for the message type in a message. Table 14 — MessageTypeEnum Enumeration MessageTypeEnum Explanation The message type Literal Explanation Info Used to inform the user about a certain fact Warning Used for warnings; warnings can lead to errors in the subsequent execution Error Used for handling errors Exception Used in case of an internal and/or unhandled exception Table 15 defines the allowed values for the status code in a message. Table 15 — Status code of response messages Generic Status Code Meaning Has Result Object HTTP Status Code Success Success No 200 SuccessCreated Successful creation of a new resource No 201 SuccessAccepted The reception of the request was successful No 202 SuccessNoContent Success with explicitly no content in the payload No 204 ClientErrorBadRequest Bad or malformed request Yes 400 ClientNotAuthorized Wrong or missing authorization credentials Yes 401 ClientForbidden Authorization has been refused Yes 403 ClientMethodNotAllow ed Method request is not allowed Yes 405 ClientErrorResourceNo tFound Resource not found Yes 404

# Page 16^page=16
prEN 18222:2025(en) 16 Generic Status Code Meaning Has Result Object HTTP Status Code ClientResourceConflict Conflict-creating resource (resource already exists) Yes 409 ServerInternalError Unexpected error Yes 500 ServerNotImplemented The server does not support the functionality to fulfil the request No 501 ServerErrorBadGatewa y Bad gateway Yes 502 8 Mappings 8.1 General This clause specified the mapping of the logical model as defined in Clause 4, Clause 5 and Clause 6 to HTTPS/REST. Table 16, Table 17 and Table 18 show the corresponding logical method names corresponding to Clause 4, Clause 5 and Clause 6, the associated HTTP methods, the REST-Resource Path, if the input parameter are provided in-path of the resource path or as payload body, as well the expected result. For update operations 0 RFC7396 can be considered for implementation. The Version-Prefix ("v1/") in REST-Path shall be replaced for further versions accordingly. The value of elementIdPath shall follow 0 RFC 9535 JSONPath. All in-path parameters like dppId should be escaped (percent encoding) within Urls. An optional "representation" query flag for the GET, POST, and PATCH methods is used that can take two values: "compressed" (default) and "full“. The "compressed“ value signalize that the client is requesting the payload containing the compressed DPP-based representation (see Clause 5.2 in prEN18223). The „full“ value will signalize that the client is requesting the payload containing the full DPP- based representation (see Annex A and B in prEN18223). If the "representation“ query is not used, the „compressed“ representation is assumed in the payload. 8.2 HTTPS/REST for Life Cycle API See Table 16 provides the core HTTPS methods of the DPP life cycle API.

# Page 17^page=17
17 Table 16 — Life Cycle API Method Name HTTP Metho d REST-Path In-path /Query Parameter Request Body Result (except status code and pagination) ReadDPPById GET v1/dpps/{dppId} DPP ID -- DPP ReadDPPByPr oductId GET v1/dppsByProductId /{productId} Product ID -- DPP (latest DPP version) ReadDPPVersi onByIdAndDat e GET v1/dppsByIdAndDate /{dppId}?date={time stamp} DigitalProduct PassportId (as in-path) and Timestamp (as query) -- DPP (based on the given date) ReadDPPIdsBy ProductIds POST v1/dppsByProductId s -- Set of Product IDs Set of DPP IDs CreateDPP POST v1/dpps -- DPP DPP ID UpdateDPPByI d PATCH v1/dpps/{dppId} DPP ID PartialDPP DPP (updated) DeleteDPPByI d DELET E v1/dpps/{dppId} DPP ID -- -- 8.3 HTTPS/REST for Register API for Register Table 17 provides the HTTPS method for the Register API that needs to be called to register a DPP. Table 17 — Register API for Register Method Name HTTP Metho d REST-Path In-path Parameter Request Body Result (except status code and pagination) RegisterProdu ctDPP POST v1/registerDPP - dppRegistryEn try object Registration ID

# Page 18^page=18
prEN 18222:2025(en) 18 8.4 HTTPS/REST for Fine Granular Life Cycle API Table 18 — Fine Granular Life Cycle API Method Name HTTP Metho d REST-Path In-path Parameter Request Body Result (except status code and pagination) ReadDataElem ent GET v1/dpps/{dppId}/ele ments/{elementIdPat h} DPP ID (dppId) and unique path to the element DataElement UpdateDataEle ment PATCH v1/dpps/{dppId}/ele ments/{elementIdPat h} DPP ID (dppId) and unique path to the element (elementPath) Data element that needs to be updated DataElement (updated)

prEN 18222:2025(en) 20 [Essential]/ [interoperability]/[…] Requirements of [Directive]/[Regulation]/[De cision] [ …] Clause(s)/sub-clause(s) of this EN Remarks/Notes 10.4 Clause 4 (all sub-clauses) functionality covers requirements for back-up operation 11.a clauses 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 provides interoperable access to dpp 11.b clauses 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 access on public data without restrictions 11.e clauses 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 functionality covers requirements for back-up operation 13.1, 13.2, 13.4, 13.5 clause 5.2 covers functionality to register new DPP on DPP registry 15.1 clauses 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 registration identifier is part of restricted data, only provided as response if authorized authority 15.4 clauses 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 EO responds dataset according authorisation WARNING — Presumption of conformity stays valid only as long as a reference to this European Standard is maintained in the list published in the Official Journal of the European Union.
